response: "to find the relationship substitute x equals 2 into the polynomial to get sixteen minus twelve plus two a plus b equals zero which simplifies to two a plus b equals negative four for the second part since x equals two is a double root the derivative f prime of x must also equal zero at x equals two the derivative is six x squared minus six x plus a so substituting x equals two gives twenty four minus twelve plus a equals zero meaning a equals negative twelve then substitute a back into the first equation to find b which results in negative twenty four plus b equals negative four so b equals twenty"
